title: verilog: modules can't override global type definitions
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```sv
typedef logic [7:0] global_t;

module top;
	typedef logic [3:0] global_t;
	global_t x = 8'h42;
	always @(*) assert(x == 4'h2);
endmodule
```

`yosys -p 'prep -top top; sat -verify -prove-asserts' test.sv`

## Expected behavior

The typedef in the module shadows the global typedef and results in `x` being 4 bits wide.

## Actual behavior

The global typedef is used for `x`, making it 8 bits wide.

## Cause

[`AST::process()`](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/4f4e70876f06738fa7dda24e01ac296fe318264a/frontends/ast/ast.cc#L1226) appends the globals to the end of a module's children, which then [iterates over all its children](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/4f4e70876f06738fa7dda24e01ac296fe318264a/frontends/ast/simplify.cc#L844) as the first  step of simplification, causing the appended global typedefs to override the module-local ones.

A quick fix would be to insert the globals *before* the module's real children, but this still causes multiple typedefs for the same name to exist in the same scope, which kind of breaks a verilog invariant. The proper solution would probably be more along the lines of adding the globals to `current_scope`, treating the global scope more like any other nested scope.
